frame_analyses.description: First instrument enters the field; trainee begins orienting and loading the needle near the right side of the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial pickup controlled; needle orientation takes a few seconds to settle before drive.

frame_analyses.description: Loaded needle brought to the Penrose and aligned for the first bite through the marked target.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle approach slightly shallow at first, small adjustment before penetration.

frame_analyses.description: Needle driven through the drain; suture progressively pulled through both marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Stable needle control; bite appears close to intended marks (exact mm limited by video resolution).

frame_analyses.description: After pull-through, trainee organizes the suture ends and sets up the first intracorporeal throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Extra tail-management time before knotting adds to overall duration.

frame_analyses.description: First throw formed and seated over the drain; configuration consistent with double-wrap surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right-sided instrument appears to lead while opposite instrument acts as post.

frame_analyses.description: Reversing single throw constructed over the first throw and tightened down onto the knot stack.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Lead appears to switch to left-sided instrument; throw lays flatter than the first.

frame_analyses.description: Final single throw formed and re-cinched with additional tightening maneuver before release.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right-sided instrument again appears to lead, consistent with alternating-hand sequence.

frame_analyses.description: Knot complete; trainee transitions to exposing suture ends for division.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Prolonged transition, suggesting extra regrasping before cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Cutter/scissors used near knot to divide suture ends inside the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting completed safely but takes longer than earlier knot throws.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments clear the field after both ends appear cut; knot seated on the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: No drain avulsion or obvious late knot failure at end of clip.

knot_assessments.notes: Double-wrap surgeon's knot led by right-sided instrument; seats without obvious immediate loosening.
Reverses direction with left-sided instrument leading, consistent with hand switch.
Returns to right-sided lead; followed by additional cinching before release.

technique_summary: V16 scored at 166.0s / 431.0 FLS. Task completed with controlled needle passage and a knot stack that appears secure at end. Economy of motion only moderate — noticeable pauses organizing tails between throws and during cutting. Tissue handling gentle, drain not avulsed. Third throw + cutting consumed ~74s (largest share of total run); biggest opportunity is faster tail organization and more decisive end-cut exposure.
improvement_suggestions: Shorten the interval between suture pull-through and the first throw by organizing the long and short tails earlier and more decisively.
Drive the needle more deliberately through the center of each mark to reduce possible millimeter deviation penalties.
Make the cutting phase more efficient by presenting both tails clearly before introducing the cutter so less regrasping is needed.
strengths: Needle passed through the drain without dropping it; bites appeared close to the intended target marks.
Completed knot stack stayed seated without obvious slippage, gapping, or drain avulsion by end of video.
comparison_notes: Paired with score_claude_V16_001 — see memory/comparisons/V16_teacher_comparison_20260407.json. ΔFLS 14.0 (moderate agreement, larger than V11/V12/V14). Time delta 9s (biggest so far). Drain-gap divergence returns on V16's 640x480 recording — Claude flags small gap (partial_small_gap), GPT-4o sees complete closure. Consistent with the monitor-resolution bias pattern (V11/V14 had it, V12 didn't).
